---
title: "DC Population"
format: html
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidycensus)
library(readxl)
library(pdftools)

conflicts_prefer(dplyr::filter)

# Prep DC population data from 1990 to 2024

# 1990-1999 data from PDF
# Citation: U.S. Census Bureau, Population Division, "Table CO-EST2001-12-00 - Time Series of Intercensal State Population Estimates: April 1, 1990 to April 1, 2000" 2002. <https://www.census.gov/data/tables/time-series/demo/popest/intercensal-1990-2000-state-and-county-totals.html>
pdf_text <- pdf_text("data/co-est2001-12-00.pdf")
lines <- str_split(pdf_text[1], "\n")[[1]]

# Process the extracted table to get DC data
# Filter for DC row and parse columns
dc_line <- lines[str_detect(lines, "District of Columbia")]
dc_values <- str_extract_all(dc_line, "\\d+,?\\d*")[[1]]

dc_population_1999 <- tibble(
	NAME = "District of Columbia",
	year = c("1990 April", 1990:2000),
	value = dc_values
)

dc_population_1999 <- dc_population_1999 |>
	filter(!year %in% c("1990 April", "2000")) |>
	mutate(value = as.integer(str_remove_all(value, ",")))

# 2000-2009 data from CSV
# Citation: U.S. Census Bureau, Population Division, "Table 1. Intercensal Estimates of the Resident Population for the United States, Regions, States, and Puerto Rico: April 1, 2000 to July 1, 2010 (ST-EST00INT-01)" 2011. <https://www.census.gov/data/tables/time-series/demo/popest/intercensal-2000-2010-state.html>

dc_population_2009 <- read_csv("data/st-est00int-01.csv", skip = 3)

dc_population_2009 <- dc_population_2009 |>
	filter(...1 == ".District of Columbia") |>
	rename(NAME = ...1) |>
	select(NAME, `2000`:`2009`) |>
	mutate(NAME = str_remove(NAME, "\\."))

dc_population_2009 <- dc_population_2009 |>
	pivot_longer(cols = -NAME, names_to = "year", values_to = "value") |>
	mutate(value = as.integer(value))

# 2010-2019 data from Excel
# Citation: U.S. Census Bureau, Population Division, "Intercensal Estimates of the Resident Population for the United States, Regions, States, District of Columbia, and Puerto Rico: April 1, 2010 to April 1, 2020 (NST-EST2020INT-POP)" 2024. <https://www.census.gov/data/tables/time-series/demo/popest/intercensal-2010-2020-state.html>
dc_population_2019 <- read_xlsx("data/nst-est2020int-pop.xlsx", skip = 3)

dc_population_2019 <- dc_population_2019 |>
	filter(...1 == ".District of Columbia") |>
	rename(NAME = ...1) |>
	select(NAME, `2010`:`2019`) |>
	mutate(NAME = str_remove(NAME, "\\."))

dc_population_2019 <- dc_population_2019 |>
	pivot_longer(cols = -NAME, names_to = "year", values_to = "value") |>
	mutate(value = as.integer(value))

# 2020-2024 data from tidycensus
# Citation: U.S. Census Bureau, Population Division, "Annual Estimates of the Resident Population for the United States, Regions, States, District of Columbia, and Puerto Rico: April 1, 2020 to July 1, 2024 (NST-EST2024-POP)" 2024. <https://www.census.gov/data/tables/time-series/demo/popest/2020s-state-total.html>

# Package citation: Walker K, Herman M (2025). _tidycensus: Load US Census Boundary and Attribute Data as 'tidyverse' and 'sf'-Ready Data Frames_. R package version 1.7.3, <https://walker-data.com/tidycensus/>.
dc_population_2024 <- get_estimates(
	geography = "state",
	state = "11",
	time_series = TRUE,
	vintage = 2024,
	variables = "POPESTIMATE",
	breakdown_labels = TRUE
)

dc_population_2024 <- dc_population_2024 |>
	select(NAME, year, value) |>
	mutate(year = as.character(year))

# Combine all data

dc_population <- bind_rows(
	dc_population_1999,
	dc_population_2009,
	dc_population_2019,
	dc_population_2024
) |>
	arrange(year)

```

# Plot the population over time

```{r}

dc_population |>
	ggplot(aes(x = as.integer(year), y = value)) +
	geom_line() +
	geom_point() +
	labs(
		title = "Population of the District of Columbia (1990-2024)",
		x = "Year",
		y = "Population"
	) +
	scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
	theme_minimal()

```
